name: get_relevant_content
description:
Get filtered relevant content from 4DA's personalized feed.

Returns content items that match the user's interests, tech stack, and learned preferences.
Items are scored based on:
- Explicit interests declared by the user
- Tech stack and domains
- ACE-detected context (recent files, projects, git activity)
- Learned preferences from past interactions

IMPORTANT for autonomous use: each item carries an `evidence_class` describing how it is grounded. The relevance_score float is NOT comparable across classes — gate actions on evidence_class, not the number:
- "osv_verified": a security advisory matching one of the user's INSTALLED dependencies — safe to act on
- "dependency_grounded": matched one of the user's declared dependencies — trustworthy
- "semantic_only": topical/embedding relevance with NO dependency edge — surface to the human, verify before acting
- "keyword_heuristic": standalone-mode keyword score (no Rust pipeline) — weakest signal

Call when the user asks what to read, what is relevant to them, or for content on a topic.

source_type.description: Filter by source type: "hackernews", "arxiv", or "reddit". Leave empty for all sources.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return. Default: 20, max: 100

since_hours.description: Only include items discovered in the last N hours. Default: 24
